clues: Although previously fatal before age 4, modern treatments of this have transformed this into a chronic disease
The nickname 'sixty-five roses' is also the unofficial symbol of the disease
This disease was described in European folkelore, 'the child will soon die whose brow tastes salty when kissed'
Believed to have evolved over 80 million years ago, this was described in the early 1500s in drawings by Leonardo da Vinci
Holding high concentrations of lymphoid tissue, this organ has been reported to grow as long as 10.24 inches long
Due to comparisons to a worm, this is frequently described as 'vermiform'
Discovered when researching compounds to relieve muscle spasms, further research into this medication's structure later aided in the discovery of an anti-depressant
Although on the BEERS list and side effects are common, this remains a widely used over the counter medication
Known for significant sedation, a typical dose can cause impairment equivalent to a blood-alcohol level of 0.1
The initial use of this type of treatment began when a surgeon from New York began injecting live bacteria directly into humans in the 1890s
Although the field has advanced significantly, the first modern success was use of the BCG vaccine for treatment of baldder cancer
Now a alphabet soup of names, this treatment has changed the field of oncology
Because of effects from WWI, the first school of this was created at Walter Reed Army Hospital
Initially a field founded and developed primarily by women, women continue to make up approximately 70% of the profession
Although early practitioners were known as 'reconstruction aides', the field has expanded to help with chronic pain, pelvic floor health, and vestibular issues
